name: Sierra Club Foundation
mission: The Sierra Club Foundation promotes climate solutions, conservation, and movement building through a powerful combination of strategic philanthropy and ...
theoryOfChange: The Sierra Club Foundation's website, specifically the 'The Foundation for a Healthy Planet' page, outlines their 'Goals & Impact' which include solving the climate crisis, securing land protections, getting people outdoors, building a movement, and shifting trillions for climate justice. It also details their 'Methods' for achieving these goals, such as partnering with donors to align financial resources with strategic outcomes and providing resources to the Sierra Club and other nonprofits for various programs. Furthermore, the 'SCF Organizational Assessment' indicates that the Foundation worked with the Sierra Club to develop a strategic framework and that its Catalytic Capital Portfolio supports programmatic efforts aligned with shared climate and social justice goals, including the Sierra Club's 2030 Strategic Framework. The Foundation also utilizes an 'Endowment Impact Benchmark' as a framework to assess its commitment to impact and sustainable investing.

scoreSummary: Sierra Club Foundation shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Platinum Rating (Endowment Impact Benchmark) (Intentional Endowments Network)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 21/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.

score_details.score_summary: Sierra Club Foundation shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierra_Club_Foundation)

all_citations.claim: The foundation manages programs like Beyond Coal and holds 17.7 months of working capital.
The organization reported Total Revenue of $108,188,731 and a program expense ratio of 86.0%.
The foundation offers various ways to give, supporting general charitable contributions or sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate score due to its exceptional financial health and program efficiency, balanced against a lower alignment for donors seeking traditional direct-aid models.
areas_for_improvement: Limited alignment with specific Muslim donor preferences or niche community needs
Heavy focus on advocacy-based impact which can be harder to quantify than direct service delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause of climate urgency is high, the alignment for specific donor groups is medium because the organization focuses on broad advocacy and policy movement building rather than direct service [1]. Donors can contribute via sadaqah, though the organization does not have specific religious giving pathways [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high impact through a strong program expense ratio of 86.0%, ensuring the vast majority of its $108,188,731 revenue directly funds environmental programs [2]. Its capacity-building model is supported by robust financial stability, evidenced by 17.7 months of working capital [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Sierra Club Foundation empowers environmental protection through strategic philanthropy, climate solutions, and movement building.
strengths: High operational efficiency with a program expense ratio of 86.0%
Strong liquidity with Working Capital of 17.7 months
baseline_narrative.summary: The Sierra Club Foundation promotes climate solutions and conservation through major initiatives such as the Beyond Coal Campaign, Our Wild America, and Outdoors for All [1]. In the most recent fiscal year, the organization reported a Total Revenue of $108,188,731 to support its advocacy and capacity-building efforts [2].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate score due to its exceptional financial health and program efficiency, balanced against a lower alignment for donors seeking traditional direct-aid models.

areas_for_improvement.context: The Foundation lacks <cite id="15">specific beneficiary counts</cite>, making it difficult to calculate the exact cost-effectiveness of its advocacy work.
The organization experienced a <cite id="16">3-year revenue CAGR of -9.5%</cite>, indicating a recent downward trend in total funding.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization currently does not meet the 20 BBB Standards for Charity Accountability.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The Foundation maintains massive net assets of <cite id="20">$167,535,669</cite> to mitigate short-term revenue drops.
risk_factors: <cite id="16">Negative 9.5% revenue growth over three years</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="16">declining revenue trends</cite> and a <cite id="18">lack of BBB accreditation</cite>.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and governance data available.

alignment.explanation: While the cause is urgent, there is <cite id="17">limited specific outreach to Muslim communities</cite> and the charity is <cite id="19">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE only</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The charity could improve alignment by <cite id="17">clearly stating how its environmental work supports underserved global Muslim populations</cite>.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this institution due to its <cite id="1">66-year track record</cite>, <cite id="10">Candid Platinum status</cite>, and <cite id="13">fully independent board</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further strengthen credibility, the charity could resolve its <cite id="18">current 0/20 status with BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>.

impact.explanation: The Foundation operates with a <cite id="7">86.0% program ratio</cite> and has a <cite id="14">documented theory of change</cite> focused on systemic climate solutions.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="15">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-per-person assessments.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Documented Theory of Change with tracked metrics

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Primary fiscal supporter for Sierra Club's national advocacy campaigns

rich_narrative.headline: The Sierra Club Foundation empowers environmental protection through strategic philanthropy, climate solutions, and movement building.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic environmental change through legal and political advocacy rather than direct service.
donor_motivations: Want to address the root causes of the climate crisis
Value long-term institutional stability and large-scale grantmaking
Prefer supporting established American environmental movements
Care about protecting public lands and clean energy transitions
giving_considerations: Donations support advocacy which has a non-linear path to impact
The organization acts as a fiscal sponsor for the Sierra Club
Financial reserves are exceptionally high, suggesting no immediate cash crisis
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those wanting to fund immediate humanitarian relief like food or medicine.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization tracks <cite id="22">two primary outcome metrics</cite> and has a clear strategic plan, though no independent RCTs are available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Foundation uses strategic grants to empower the Sierra Club's advocacy, aiming to transition the US to a clean energy economy.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In advocacy, tracking policy wins and energy transition milestones is critical to ensure funding leads to actual carbon reduction.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While reserves are high, the <cite id="16">9.5% decline in revenue</cite> suggests a need for new donor pipelines to sustain large-scale grants.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by <cite id="11">nearly 17.7 months of working capital</cite>, far exceeding the <cite id="21">78.7% peer program ratio median</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on legal advocacy and climate policy
similar_organizations.name: Natural Resources Defense Council

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses more heavily on environmental litigation
similar_organizations.name: Earthjustice

similar_organizations.differentiator: Emphasis on scientific research to drive policy change
similar_organizations.name: Union of Concerned Scientists

strengths.detail: The Foundation maintains <cite id="11">17.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer against economic volatility.
With a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 86.0%</cite>, the charity meets the 75% industry standard for advocacy groups.
The organization is governed by a <cite id="12">board of 30 members</cite> with <cite id="13">100% independent voting membership</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Sierra Club Foundation excels in financial stewardship and long-term sustainability. Its <cite id="11">working capital ratio of 17.7 months</cite> is nearly double the typical nonprofit reserve, ensuring that long-term advocacy campaigns like <cite id="9">Beyond Coal</cite> remain funded during revenue fluctuations. Furthermore, its <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite> demonstrates a highly effective development operation that maximizes the impact of every dollar donated. This financial health is paired with a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, indicating that the Foundation proactively shares its <cite id="14">theory of change and outcome metrics</cite> with the public.
rich_narrative.summary: The Sierra Club Foundation, <cite id="1">founded in 1960</cite>, serves as the fiscal engine for one of America's most influential environmental movements. The organization focuses on <cite id="2">climate solutions, conservation, and movement building</cite> through strategic philanthropy. In the 2023 fiscal year, the Foundation reported <cite id="3">Total Revenue of $89,068,739</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">$85,543,074 in public contributions</cite>. Unlike direct-service charities, this organization operates as a capacity builder, <cite id="5">distributing over $281 million in total grants</cite> to support environmental advocacy. Its primary beneficiary is the Sierra Club, which received <cite id="6">multiple grants exceeding $85 million each</cite> for domestic programs. The Foundation maintains a lean operational model, ensuring that <cite id="7">86.0% of its expenses go directly to programs</cite>. This efficiency is supported by a <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than the $0.25 industry benchmark. While the Foundation does not provide direct humanitarian aid, its work in <cite id="9">clean power advocacy and nature protection</cite> addresses the systemic causes of climate-driven displacement and health crises. The organization holds a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, reflecting a high level of public disclosure regarding its goals and metrics.
